title: 9 Herbal Creams For Swollen Gums
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to soothe swollen gums. When you apply a cream to your gums, it helps to reduce swelling and ease pain. But did you know that drinking herbal teas can also help to relieve swollen gums? This is because herbal teas work from the inside out, reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection that's causing the swelling.  Herbal teas like Echinacea purpurea and Eucalyptus globulus have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in your gums. Echinacea purpurea, in particular, is known for its ability to boost your immune system, which can help to fight off infections that cause swollen gums.  Another herb that can help is Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in your gums. It's also known for its ability to reduce nausea and fever, which can be helpful if you're experiencing these symptoms along with swollen gums.  Drinking herbal teas can have many benefits for your life beyond just relieving swollen gums. For example, they can help to boost your immune system, reduce inflammation, and even help to manage stress and anxiety. They're also a great alternative to over-the-counter medications, which can have negative side effects.  When you drink herbal teas, the active ingredients in the herbs are absorbed into your bloodstream and can reach your gums, helping to reduce swelling and ease pain. This can be especially helpful if you're experiencing swollen gums due to a cold sore or a dental infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains bioactive compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in gums. Alkylamides in particular have been found to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals, such as prostaglandins, that contribute to gum inflammation. Caffeic acid derivatives have antioxidant properties, which help protect gum tissues from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting gum tissues, Echinacea purpurea creams may be beneficial for swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea roots, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (optional).
Dry the Echinacea roots in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours. Allow them to cool completely.
In a double boiler, melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat. Stir until the beeswax dissolves.
Add the dried Echinacea roots to the melted oil mixture and simmer for 30 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Add the vitamin E oil and essential oil (if using) to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and beta-pinene. These oils have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce swelling and fight infection in swollen gums. Eucalyptol, the main component of the oil, has a numbing effect that can ease pain and discomfort. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of eucalyptus globulus help control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections. When applied topically, the active constituents in eucalyptus globulus creams can help soothe and calm swollen gums, promoting healthy healing and recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Add the shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden completely before use. Apply a small amount to swollen gums as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains the active constituents gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and LOX. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of gingerol and shogaol also help relieve pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Zingiber officinale creams, including gingerols and sesquiterpenes, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and promote healing. Regular application of Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate swollen gums by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Then, add the Zingiber officinale essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, your Zingiber officinale cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains compounds like tannins and ursolic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and fight infection in swollen gums. Tannins also have astringent properties, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. The antioxidant properties of ursolic acid help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. The combination of these properties in Salvia officinalis creams makes it a potential remedy for swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil over low heat and add the Salvia officinalis leaves.
Let the mixture simmer for 20 minutes, then remove from heat.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the leaves.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter into the cooled mixture and whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains active constituents like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling in the gums by constricting blood vessels and preventing the release of chemicals that cause inflammation. The antiseptic properties of menthol and other constituents also help to prevent infection and promote healing in swollen gums. Additionally, menthone has a local anesthetic effect, which can help to numb the pain in swollen gums and make them more comfortable. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Mentha x piperita creams can help to alleviate swollen gums and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons Mentha x piperita essential oil, and 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Remove melted mixture from heat and stir in Mentha x piperita essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour mixture into a small tin or container and let cool until hardened.
Use cooled Mentha x piperita cream as needed to soothe swollen gums, applying a small amount with a clean finger or cotton swab.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight infection in swollen gums. The flavonoids in Calendula officinalis creams, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate gum swelling. The triterpenoid saponins, including calenduloside and diosphenol, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that contribute to swollen gums. By reducing inflammation and fighting infection, Calendula officinalis creams can help soothe and heal swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the coconut oil and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
Add shea butter, dried Calendula officinalis flowers, and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool. Apply the cream to swollen gums as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains the active constituents glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling in gums by inhibiting the production of inflammatory enzymes. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to have a potent anti-inflammatory effect by blocking the action of a key enzyme involved in inflammation. The flavonoids present in the cream also have antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the gums from damage, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, and 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated. Remove from heat.
Stir in glycerin and Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder. Let the mixture cool until it reaches a consistency similar to whipped cream.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely before use. Apply a small amount to swollen gums as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in swollen gums. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to the inflammation and swelling associated with gum inflammation. The flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that help to protect the gums from oxidative stress and promote healing. By applying Achillea millefolium creams to swollen gums, the active constituents can work together to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine dried flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat. Add the strained mixture and vitamin E oil. Stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, apply a small amount to swollen gums as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The antiseptic properties of Melissa officinalis also prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause infection and further inflammation. Additionally, the creams' soothing effect helps calm irritated tissues and promotes healing. By applying Melissa officinalis creams to swollen gums, you can help alleviate discomfort and promote a healthy gum environment.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for swollen gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Melissa officinalis leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 1 hour to remove moisture.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Melissa officinalis leaves to the melted oil and beeswax mixture, and stir well.
Remove from heat, let it cool, then mix in the vitamin E oil and pour the mixture into a container. Let it set and solidify before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent swollen gums from forming. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and soothe gums. These teas can also help kill bacteria in the mouth, which can cause gum infections. Regular consumption may promote healthy gums and prevent swelling.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm swollen gums. However, drinking them every day may not be the best idea. Some herbs can be too strong and may irritate the gums further. It's best to limit your consumption to when you need it most, like when your gums are really sore.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce swollen gums. Some people notice a difference in a few days to a week, while others may take longer. The results can vary depending on the type of herbal tea and how often you drink it. You may need to try different teas to find what works best for you.
faq_time: For swollen gums, try drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the tea's soothing properties to work throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for swollen gums. Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba and garlic may increase bleeding risk, while others like chamomile and peppermint can slow down blood clotting. If you're taking medications for gum problems, talk to your pharmacist about potential interactions with herbal teas you're consuming.
faq_pregnant: If you have swollen gums while pregnant, it's generally safe to try herbal teas for some relief. However, always check the ingredients and avoid teas with ingredients like sage, pennyroyal, and blue cohosh, which aren't recommended during pregnancy. Look for teas with soothing herbs like peppermint or chamomile.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful relief for swollen gums, but check the ingredients first. Some herbs, like sage and peppermint, are safe for breastfeeding moms. However, others like chamomile and licorice root may pass into breast milk and affect the baby, so it's best to choose herbs that are specifically labeled as safe for nursing mothers.
title_2: 9 Herbal Creams for Pregnant Women with Swollen Gums
